summary: Fiery first set, storm‑charged second set with Watchtower → China Doll
blurb: The show opens tight with a scorching Sugaree and Stagger Lee before rolling into a thunderous second set highlighted by the All Along The Watchtower → China Doll transition and a soaring Turn On Your Lovelight. Jerry delivers soaring solos throughout, while Phil lays down funky bass on Lovelight. The encore Liberty lifts the night to an uplifting close.
review: June 18, 1993 – Soldier Field, Chicago. From the first note of “Hell In A Bucket” the band was locked in, launching into a blistering “Sugaree” that set the tone for a high‑octane first set. Jerry’s lead work on “Stagger Lee” and “Queen Jane Approximately” was described as soaring and spot‑on, while Phil’s bass turned funky during “Turn On Your Lovelight”. The second set rode the summer storm with a seamless segue from “All Along The Watchtower” into an electrifying “China Doll”, a moment repeatedly praised for its dramatic solo and rich texture. Though the Drums/Space segment drew mixed reactions, it served as an atmospheric bridge to the soaring climax of “Turn On Your Lovelight”. The night closed on a bright note with “Liberty,” leaving the crowd uplifted after a rain‑soaked, thunder‑enhanced marathon.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All four recordings use positive language, repeatedly highlighting soaring Jerry solos, strong band chemistry, and standout moments like the Watchtower → China Doll transition. No negative comments about performance appear, indicating consistent praise across sources.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard source with clear, well‑balanced fidelity that captures the storm‑charged performance and all highlighted songs.

key_highlights: Scorching “Sugaree” and “Stagger Lee” open a tight first set.
"All Along The Watchtower → China Doll" sequence delivers a dramatic, soaring solo.
Second‑set jams ride the stormy atmosphere, culminating in an uplifting "Turn On Your Lovelight".

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring solos on Sugaree, Stagger Lee, and the Watchtower → China Doll transition; tone described as spot‑on and dramatically dramatic.
band_performance.Phil: Provides a solid bass foundation throughout; gets especially funky during Lovelight and adds texture to the extended jams.
band_performance.Bob: Raspier vocal timbre but still powerful; strong presence on the encore and contributes additional texture alongside Phil.
band_performance.Mickey: Dual drumming with Billy creates an atmospheric Drums/Space segment that some found aimless yet tied into the stormy vibe.
band_performance.Billy: Works with Mickey to shape the rhythmic landscape; his contributions help drive the second‑set intensity despite mixed feelings about the Space portion.
